name: Debt Solutions Group
description_short: Southern California bankruptcy law firm offering Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 filing with low entry fees ($99 start), fixed pricing, and flexible payment plans across four locations.
description_long:
Debt Solution Law Group is a California-based bankruptcy law practice founded in 2005, headquartered in San Diego with satellite offices in Chula Vista, El Cajon, Escondido, and Serra Mesa. The firm is led by Steven E. Cowen, a USD Law School graduate (cum laude, 1987) who is a State Bar of California member and belongs to the National Association of Consumer Bankruptcy Attorneys. The firm reports serving over 2,200 clients since its inception.

The company specializes in personal and business bankruptcy filings, primarily Chapter 7 and Chapter 13 cases. They advertise fixed fees with a "lowest fees guaranteed" price match policy, allowing clients to initiate cases with a $99 payment and spread remaining fees across typically five installments. Critically, they file cases after the first payment rather than requiring full upfront payment. They offer same-day filing when needed at no additional charge. Free initial attorney consultations are provided to assess eligibility and explore alternatives.

Differentiators include flexible payment structures (customizable beyond the standard five-payment plan), bilingual services (English/Spanish), extended office hours (9 a.m. to 8 p.m.), and explicit positioning against "bait and switch" tactics. The firm emphasizes immediate relief from wage garnishments, bank levies, lawsuits, repossessions, foreclosures, and creditor harassment through the automatic stay mechanism triggered by filing.

A significant limitation is that the website provides no detail on case complexity handling, success rates, or outcomes. The "2,200 clients since 2005" claim is unverified. The firm appears to operate primarily in Southern California, limiting accessibility for consumers elsewhere. No information is available regarding Chapter 13 plan confirmation success rates, average case duration, or fees for complex matters beyond the entry-level $99 promotion.
pros: Low barrier to entry: $99 initiates case filing without requiring full upfront payment
Fixed fee structure with price-match guarantee against competitors' written quotes
File after first payment rather than requiring full payment before work begins
Same-day filing available at no extra charge when needed
Lead attorney (Steven E. Cowen) is cum laude law school graduate and NACBA member
Four convenient Southern California office locations with extended hours (9 a.m.–8 p.m.) and free parking
Free initial attorney consultation to assess bankruptcy eligibility and alternatives
cons: Website provides no information on case outcomes, success rates, or Chapter 13 plan confirmation statistics
Limited geographic service area (Southern California only) despite national bankruptcy issues
Unverified claim of '2,200 clients since 2005' with no third-party corroboration
No transparent fee schedule published online; competitive pricing only revealed upon consultation
No discussion of alternative debt relief options or when bankruptcy may not be appropriate
best_for: Southern California residents facing wage garnishments, lawsuits, or foreclosure seeking immediate relief
Consumers with limited liquid assets who need to spread legal fees over multiple months
Business owners requiring both personal and business bankruptcy reorganization
Spanish-speaking debtors needing bilingual legal representation
diagnosis: Best for Southern California residents in active financial crisis (garnishments, levies, lawsuits, foreclosure) who need immediate relief and cannot pay bankruptcy fees upfront. Main caveat: no published outcomes data, success metrics, or fee transparency—all details require in-person or phone consultation, making cost/outcome comparison difficult before engagement.
services: Lawsuit defense and creditor harassment cessation
Free initial bankruptcy eligibility consultation with licensed attorney
Flexible multi-payment fee plans (customizable beyond standard structure)
